page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8369B cores running at 2.7 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng CPU test scored 1855.08, and the Geekbench single-core score reached 1553.0, both falling into the average category. However, multi-core performance is constrained, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1808.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 1896.44, both rated as poor. Memory bandwidth is also limited, measuring 14.09 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 12.92 GB/sec for large block write operations.

Given its hardware profile, the ecs.g7t.large offers an entry-level dedicated resource tier. It is suitable for lightweight general-purpose workloads that do not require high memory bandwidth or parallel processing power, such as basic web serving, single-threaded text processing, or small-scale application hosting. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which should be factored into the overall resource architecture.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7t.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Utilizing the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 4.0 GB of memory per core and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU resources included. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1553.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1855.08. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively limited, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 1808.0 and a large-block read memory bandwidth of 14.09 GB/sec. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and small-scale application hosting where dedicated CPU resources are required without massive parallel processing demands.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86 virtual machine offering balanced memory-to-CPU allocation for entry-level workloads and basic web serving.
